item.title: EU MiCA entered full enforcement July 1, barring unlicensed crypto firms from serving clients across all 27 EU member states
JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs launched limited production blockchain trades of tokenized stocks and Treasuries on DTCC's ComposerX
Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, and BlackRock joined 140+ firms backing Open USD, a new stablecoin that allows members to retain reserve earnings
Circle received OCC approval July 13 to establish a federally chartered national trust bank for USDC and digital asset custody
OCC issued a proposed rule implementing the GENIUS Act, setting federal requirements for payment stablecoin issuance by national banks
Crypto protocols lost $35M in multiple attacks on July 23, including a $7.54M Ethereum bridge drain and a $3.86M staking contract exploit
SEC set July 2026 deadlines for crypto rules covering token offerings, broker-dealer custody standards, and trading venue market structure
Crypto losses from hacks reached $1.3 billion in 2026 across fewer but more targeted attacks; Q2 alone recorded $763M across 207 incidents
Wanchain's Cardano-to-BNB Chain bridge was exploited July 21 for approximately $10M, with 515 million NIGHT tokens drained from the treasury
Ethereum ETFs drew $9.3 billion in net inflows in July and August, accelerating institutional allocation following GENIUS Act passage
SEC and CFTC signed a coordination MoU in March 2026 and jointly clarified how federal securities laws apply to digital assets
CFTC staff guidance allows futures commission merchants and clearing organizations to accept tokenized assets as eligible collateral
Fidelity launched a Reserves Digital Fund at 0.18% expense ratio with a GENIUS Act mandate and a planned blockchain share class
